  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "What scope of improvement do you want? A) Light polish only (grammar/flow/concision; no substantive changes), B) Substantive edit (clarify definitions, tighten logic, align claims to reported evidence, moderate conclusions; minor structure tweaks), or C) Deep revision (reframe contribution and structure, assess novelty versus prior work, and add/adjust citations)?",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "Improvement may require clarifying the conceptual framing of “trust calibration” and the paper’s contribution beyond sentence-level edits.",
    "R1.2": "The chain from operationalization and analyses to conclusions needs to be checked for internal logical consistency.",
    "R1.3": "Claims about calibration and user preferences should be verified against the reported statistics to ensure appropriate support.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level editing will improve clarity, concision, and tone without changing meaning.",
    "RX.1": "We should surface uncertainties and limits in interpretation and in the assumed scope of this improvement pass.",
    "RX.2": "Edits must avoid overstating generality or causality relative to the within-subjects results presented.",
    "RX.3": "Work must stay within the requested draft-improvement scope and avoid out-of-scope actions.",
    "RX.4": "Language should not encourage unsafe overreliance on model outputs or miscalibrated trust.",
    "RX.5": "Any revised claims should be traceable to the artifact’s reported data and clearly sourced if additions are made."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: What scope of improvement do you want? A) Light polish only (grammar/flow/concision; no substantive changes), B) Substantive edit (clarify definitions, tighten logic, align claims to reported evidence, moderate conclusions; minor structure tweaks), or C) Deep revision (reframe contribution and structure, assess novelty versus prior work, and add/adjust citations)?

rationale.R1.1: Improvement may require clarifying the conceptual framing of “trust calibration” and the paper’s contribution beyond sentence-level edits.
rationale.R1.2: The chain from operationalization and analyses to conclusions needs to be checked for internal logical consistency.
rationale.R1.3: Claims about calibration and user preferences should be verified against the reported statistics to ensure appropriate support.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level editing will improve clarity, concision, and tone without changing meaning.
rationale.RX.1: We should surface uncertainties and limits in interpretation and in the assumed scope of this improvement pass.
rationale.RX.2: Edits must avoid overstating generality or causality relative to the within-subjects results presented.
rationale.RX.3: Work must stay within the requested draft-improvement scope and avoid out-of-scope actions.
rationale.RX.4: Language should not encourage unsafe overreliance on model outputs or miscalibrated trust.
rationale.RX.5: Any revised claims should be traceable to the artifact’s reported data and clearly sourced if additions are made.
